req.title: Create patient (boundary)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-026 mandates creation of patient resources with validated demographics, rejection of missing mandatory elements (422 error), and boundary testing on timestamps and numeric fields. The test suite applies Equivalence Partitioning to partition valid/invalid demographic sets, Boundary Value Analysis for edge cases on dates and numeric identifiers, and Negative Path testing for missing mandatory fields. Each test is deterministic, self-contained, and traces to specific outcome requirements.

tests.name: test_create_patient_valid_demographics_positive
actions: POST /Patient with complete demographics: given name, family name, birth date (valid ISO8601), gender (male), contact (email, phone)
expected: response body contains patient resource with id
trace: R-HLT-026: creation with mandatory elements present
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_create_patient_valid_demographics_positive():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"given": ["John"], "family": "Doe"}],
        "birthDate": "1980-05-15",
        "gender": "male",
        "telecom": [
            {"system": "email", "value": "john.doe@example.com"},
            {"system": "phone", "value": "+1-555-0100"}
        ]
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Patient ID not in response"
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert "Location" in response.headers or response.status_code == 201

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name_negative
actions: POST /Patient with omitted 'name' field but other mandatory fields present (birthDate, gender)
expected: error response body includes validation error for missing name
trace: R-HLT-026: creation with missing mandatory element shall be rejected
R-HLT-026: error pathway 422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name_negative():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "birthDate": "1980-05-15",
        "gender": "male",
        "telecom": [{"system": "email", "value": "test@example.com"}]
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "issue" in body or "error" in body, "Error details missing"
    assert "name" in str(body).lower(), "Error should reference missing name field"

tests.name: test_create_patient_boundary_birthdate_max_age
actions: POST /Patient with birthDate at boundary: 1900-01-01 (oldest reasonable patient, 120+ years)
expected: patient resource stored with birthDate exactly 1900-01-01
numeric age calculation does not overflow
trace: R-HLT-026: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-026: patient resource stored with extreme but valid date
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_patient_boundary_birthdate_max_age():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"given": ["Ancient"], "family": "Patient"}],
        "birthDate": "1900-01-01",
        "gender": "female"
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert body["birthDate"] == "1900-01-01", "Boundary date not preserved"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_create_patient_boundary_birthdate_future_rejection
preconditions: system validation enforces no future birth dates
actions: POST /Patient with birthDate in future (e.g., +5 years from today)
expected: error indicates invalid birthDate (future not allowed)
trace: R-HLT-026: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-026: creation rejected for invalid temporal boundary
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_create_patient_boundary_birthdate_future_rejection():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    future_date = (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=365*5)).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"given": ["Future"], "family": "Born"}],
        "birthDate": future_date,
        "gender": "male"
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {response.status_code}"
    assert "birthDate" in str(response.json()).lower() or "date" in str(response.json()).lower()

tests.name: test_create_patient_boundary_numeric_identifier_max
actions: POST /Patient with identifier containing maximum numeric value (e.g., 9999999999)
expected: identifier stored exactly as provided without truncation
trace: R-HLT-026: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-026: patient resource stored with boundary numeric identifier
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_patient_boundary_numeric_identifier_max():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <clinician_token>", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"given": ["Max"], "family": "Identifier"}],
        "birthDate": "1970-01-01",
        "gender": "other",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:example:mrn", "value": "9999999999"}]
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert body["identifier"][0]["value"] == "9999999999"

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_gender_negative
actions: POST /Patient with name and birthDate but omitted 'gender' field
expected: error response indicates missing or invalid gender
trace: R-HLT-026: creation with missing mandatory element shall be rejected
